# aiflow — full reference for LLMs

> Self-contained explanation of what aiflow is and how it works, for AI assistants and context7.
> aiflow turns any repository into a governed, AI-driven software-delivery pipeline for Claude Code.
> Version 0.2.0 · MIT · https://github.com/Cyber93de/aiflow · docs https://cyber93de.github.io/aiflow/

## What aiflow is
A cross-platform CLI (POSIX bash + a Windows PowerShell launcher) that bootstraps and governs
Claude Code projects. It wires Claude Code to durable task memory, two-layer code memory, specialist
agents, team collaboration, and token savings — with one command. It is project-scoped (secrets and
settings live in the project, never globally), token-based (never OAuth for git hosts), and
vendor-neutral (your own Anthropic API key or Claude Code OAuth token). It vendors nothing; it
installs/invokes external tools.

## How it works (the pipeline)
1. `aiflow init` (or `change-settings`) runs an interactive Q&A and writes `.aiflow/config.json` —
   the single source of truth.
2. `lib/apply.sh` reads that config and idempotently RENDERS: `.mcp.json` (enabled MCP servers +
   the git-host MCP for your remote), git branching + hooks (git only), the Beads↔host sync config,
   `.claude/memory/*` (project-aim, dev-environment, memory-policy), `.aiflow/router-config.json`
   (Ollama/cost providers), `.aiflow/team-prefs.json`, and `.aiflow/bd-close-sync.sh`.
3. `lib/install-deps.sh` installs only the enabled tools (user-space).

Every script a project invokes on its own (Claude Code hooks, `.aiflow/*` checks, `docker/run.*`)
ships as a `.sh` + `.ps1` pair; `apply.sh` picks the interpreter per `dev.os` when writing
`.claude/settings.json`'s hook commands — Windows projects need no Git-Bash for their own
hooks/checks. `aiflow update` self-updates the CLI install; `aiflow project-update` refreshes one
project's mechanical scripts from the installed templates (each project stamps the aiflow version
it was generated with in `meta.aiflowVersion` and is prompted to upgrade when it falls behind).

## Code memory (the differentiator)
Two complementary indexes plus durable task memory. The agent routes each question to the cheapest
source that answers it (written to `.claude/memory/memory-policy.md`):
- Beads (`bd`) — current task, dependencies, decisions, session state (Dolt-backed, survives compaction).
- memory files — durable prose facts.
- graphify (MCP) — STRUCTURAL code graph: where a symbol is defined, who calls it, dependency direction.
- cocoindex-code / `ccc` (MCP) — SEMANTIC RAG: "find code about concept X"; AST-aware, local
  embeddings (sentence-transformers, no API key), ~70% fewer tokens than reading files; incremental.
- context7 (MCP) — external, version-correct library/framework docs.
Rule: never scan whole files first; use graph + RAG to locate the few relevant chunks, then open only
those. `aiflow index` refreshes BOTH indexes (graphify build + ccc index).

## Remote hosts (token-based)
`remote.type` ∈ github | github-enterprise | gitlab | gitlab-self | bitbucket | forgejo | gitea |
custom | none. The matching host MCP is wired automatically with the base URL threaded in
(GITHUB_HOST / GITLAB_API_URL / GITEA_URL). Token comes from `.env` via `remote.tokenEnv`
(e.g. GITHUB_TOKEN, GITLAB_TOKEN, GIT_REMOTE_TOKEN). No OAuth for git hosts.

## Models
- Claude access: `claude.auth` = apikey (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) or oauth (CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN).
- Ollama: local models, no key; `aiflow ollama add|pull|list`; qwen3-coder recommended. Selected
  models are written into `.aiflow/router-config.json` and used via `aiflow shell --router`.
- More/cloud models via claude-code-router (~/.claude-code-router/config.json): DeepSeek, OpenRouter, Gemini.

## Team collaboration
Beads issues live in a shared Dolt database synced over `refs/dolt/data` on the git remote — one issue
graph, no extra server. Rules: session-start auto-pull (`bd dolt pull` hook), atomic claiming
(`bd ready --claim` / `bd update --claim`, prevents double-grab), pull-before-push
(`aiflow sync`, `aiflow close-sync`), never force-push, conflicts resolved via `bd dolt pull`.

## Agents
Delivery: architect, planner, implementer (senior engineer: mandatory pre-analysis of architecture
fit/effort/complexity before code, targeted refactoring, SOLID/DRY/KISS/YAGNI, testable by design,
proven frameworks + design patterns over self-built, PO-level questions with recorded decisions),
reviewer (architect AND quality gate in one: architecture integrity + ADRs, design, tech debt,
vulnerabilities, concurrency, breaking changes, plus an objective release checklist; PASS/CHANGES
REQUIRED; suggestions persisted as beads), tester (test/QA engineer: negative/edge/boundary/
exception/invalid-input + test-quality audit; runs adaptively on high risk/complexity).
All delivery agents: production-ready output only (low-maturity tech flagged by reviewer+tester),
small classes/KISS (divide & conquer + interfaces; utility-lib overloads excepted), monolith
avoidance (service-ready seams), state-of-the-art check (SOAP-vs-REST, XML-vs-JSON, legacy MQ
questioned as PO decisions), deliberate Redis/SQLite/Elasticsearch consideration for perf/search.
Audit (file Beads issues): security-advisor, quality-check, dependency-auditor, test-gap-advisor,
performance-advisor, docs-sync, accessibility-checker (aiflow a11y-check: strict WCAG 2.2 AA ->
[accessibility] beads + recommends axe-core/Pa11y/Lighthouse CI for the E2E suite; not in the
loop), requirements-check (report only), modernization-advisor (aiflow modernize-check: brownfield
modernisation concepts as report .aiflow/modernization-report.md for the architect — EOL stacks,
monolith->microservices strangler-fig, legacy->REST/JSON+cloud-native eventing, svn->git, missing
unit/BDD/E2E frameworks; report only, not in the loop). Brownfield: onboarder (learns the codebase into
memory/CLAUDE.md/arc42 and proposes a project aim from its understanding — user must confirm it).
Shipped agents are deliberately generic (strong universal base config, ~70-80% less setup than
blank Claude); customise them per project in .claude/agents/*.md. The project aim
(.claude/memory/project-aim.md + CLAUDE.md §1, set via init/change-settings) tunes Claude to the
project — 2-4 sentences: what, for whom, target architecture, quality bar. Slash skills:
/intake-issue, /decompose, /plan-epic, /implement [bead] [ralph|no-ralph], /review-ac, /arch, the
audit commands, /onboard, /explain, /standup. Autonomy: the Ralph loop (interactive / headless
`aiflow ralph` / CI); if not specified, the implementer decides from its pre-analysis.

## Quality, git, releases
Google style for all languages; Conventional Commits; pre-commit/commit-msg/pre-push enforcement
hooks. Quality gates on every implementation: static analysis (tool or agent-performed — no code
smells), >80 % line coverage + all non-static methods tested, unit + BDD end-to-end tests always
(integration/system where sensible; BDD for E2E/system/acceptance), leveled logging mandatory;
REST APIs are versioned from day one (/api/v1/...; breaking -> new version + deprecation window)
and properly secured — Basic Auth is insufficient: OAuth2/OIDC, short-lived JWT, or managed API
keys with rotation; authorisation per endpoint. REST endpoints ship IDE-testable .http files
(host/port/test credentials from .env). Ralph loop: implementer decides automatically from its
pre-analysis; manual triggers win (/implement <bead> ralph|no-ralph, or a directive written into
the issue itself). Token framing: saving is a goal but partially achieved per task due to quality
rules; net win = production-ready on first pass, no re-prompting/rework. Database
rules (CLAUDE.md §3c): NEW data models >=3NF, real foreign keys, junction tables for m:n, NOT NULL
default, CHECK/UNIQUE constraints, precise types, no magic values, only necessary indexes, lookup
tables, deliberate cascades, soft delete where needed (R1-R20); EXISTING schemas are handled with
care (B1-B8 cautionary guidance): they may be shared by other projects and must support rollback
to older app versions — never restructure/re-key/re-type/merge/split/normalise as a side effect;
note improvement potential as recommendation beads; commissioned schema changes are high-risk work
(external-consumer check, backward-compat/rollback plan, versioned migration). Uncommissioned
structural changes in a feature diff = review BLOCKER. Branching models: simple | gitflow | none, PR-only, auto-release, SemVer/CalVer; enforced by
hooks + `aiflow protect` + `aiflow release`.

## Token optimization
caveman (terse output, ~75% fewer output tokens; code/commits/security stay normal) and rtk
(CLI-output filtering, 60–90% fewer) are ON by default; graph/RAG retrieval; cheap/local model
routing. Measure with `aiflow cost` (ccusage).

## Containers & CI/CD
Headless container runs via `docker/run.sh` using Podman OR Docker (auto-detected; `AIFLOW_CONTAINER`
override). CI/CD is GitHub Actions: `ci.yml` (validate + dry-run build), `release.yml` (tag + per-OS
archives on VERSION bump), `pages.yml` (deploy docs). No Dagger.

## Key commands
init · install-deps · change-settings · shell [--router] · sync [pull|push|both] · close-sync <id> ·
ollama [pull|add|list] · index · ralph "<prompt|bead>" · onboard · security-check / quality-check /
requirements-check / dependency-check / test-gap / perf-check / docs-check · release [--push] ·
protect · cost · doctor · upgrade · update · project-update · version.

## Config schema (essentials)
.aiflow/config.json fields: caveman{enabled,mode}, rtk{enabled}, router{enabled}, graphify{enabled},
taskmaster{enabled}, mcp{filesystem,context7,cocoindex}, memory{enabled,graph,intensity=off|light|
normal|aggressive}, claude{auth}, vcs{system=git|svn|none}, remote{type,baseUrl,api,tokenEnv,mcp},
sync{askOnClose,pullOnStart}, ollama{enabled,url,models}, teamPrefs{enabled,codeStyle},
project{aim,architecture}, dev{os,ide}, git{model,strict,prOnly,autoRelease,versionStrategy,releaseTags,chore},
meta{aiflowVersion} (stamped at init; compared against the installed CLI's VERSION to offer
`aiflow project-update`).

## Attribution
aiflow builds on Claude Code (Anthropic), Beads, Dolt, graphify, CocoIndex/cocoindex-code, Context7,
claude-task-master, claude-code-router, Ollama, rtk, ccusage, jq, uv, and the MCP servers ecosystem.
See THIRD_PARTY_LICENSES.md. aiflow is independent and not affiliated with or endorsed by them.
